meaning: The root عسي (Esy) primarily denotes hope or expectation, often with an element of difficulty or uncertainty. It can also imply a near occurrence or the act of being about to do something.

name: غلب
meaning: The root غلب (ghalaba) primarily means to overcome, conquer, or gain ascendancy over someone or something. It implies prevailing in power, force, or influence.

name: دري
meaning: The root د-ر-ي primarily means to know, perceive, or be aware of something, often implying a degree of seeking or finding out. It can also convey the sense of being skilled or experienced in a matter.

name: مدد
meaning: The root مَدَد primarily signifies extension, stretching, or prolonging something, whether physically or metaphorically. It can also mean to supply or provide assistance, often in a continuous flow.

name: وثق
meaning: The root وثق (wthq) conveys securing oneself or something, often through a bond or firm action. It implies taking precautions, ensuring stability, or establishing trust.

name: بدو
meaning: The root "bdw" primarily means "to appear" or "become manifest." It also describes an opinion arising or changing, or people going out to the desert.

name: حيث
meaning: حَيْثُ (haythu) is an indeclinable adverb of place, meaning "where." It typically precedes a proposition (a phrase with a verb or noun). While primarily denoting place, it can sometimes signify "when.

name: هود
meaning: The root هود (hwd) primarily relates to becoming Jewish or teaching Judaism. It also describes gentle, slow movement or speech, like a soft voice or leisurely pace.

name: رفع
meaning: The root "رفع" (rafaʿa) primarily means to raise, elevate, or uplift something, often in contrast to lowering it.

name: وسع
meaning: The root وسع (w-s-ʿ) conveys the idea of becoming ample, abundant, or expansive. It describes widening, spreading out, or taking a broad range in affairs, bounty, or even the meaning of words.

name: صير
meaning: The root صير (Syr) primarily means "to become" or "to attain a state/condition." It also signifies "to return to a place" or "to come to an end.

name: صرف
meaning: The root S-R-F generally means to turn something away, back, or aside from its course, or to shift it from one state to another.

name: نصب
meaning: The root نَصَبَ (naṣaba) primarily means to set up, erect, or raise something. It also describes a specific type of Arabic chanting or singing.

name: كيد
meaning: The root كيد (kyd) primarily means to deceive, beguile, or circumvent someone, often with ill intent or clandestinely.

name: أفك
meaning: The root أفك (Afk) primarily means to turn something away or back, often implying deceit or falsehood. It can describe being turned away from truth or good, or someone lying or causing others to lie.

name: عمي
meaning: The root عمي primarily means blindness, both physical and metaphorical. It refers to being unable to see or perceive, often implying a lack of guidance or understanding.

name: برر
meaning: The root برر (brr) primarily signifies being good, righteous, or pious. It encompasses truthfulness, extensive goodness, and dutifulness, especially towards parents.

name: خلص
meaning: The root خَلَص (khalaṣa) refers to something becoming clear, pure, or refined. It's often used for clarifying butter by removing impurities, or for the best, most essential part of something.

name: شرر
meaning: The root شَرَر primarily refers to sparks of fire, or small, scattered pieces that fly off from a burning object.

name: عفو
meaning: The root عفو (Efw) primarily means to efface or obliterate, like traces or dwellings. From this, it extends to forgiving sins, meaning to efface them.

name: ظلل
meaning: The root ظَلَلَ primarily means to shade or cover, providing protection from the sun or heat. It can also imply being in the shade, or something that is dark or obscure.

name: وري
meaning: The root وري primarily means to conceal or hide something, especially fire or embers. It also refers to something being ignited or coming forth, like fire from wood.

name: خفي
meaning: The root خفي (khfy) primarily means to be hidden, concealed, or secret. It can refer to something that is not apparent or known, whether intentionally or unintentionally.

name: جنب
meaning: The root جنب (jnb) primarily refers to the side of a person or object, extending from the armpit to the flank. It can also mean a general side, region, or quarter.

name: قسم
meaning: Qismah refers to a division or apportionment of things into shares. It can also mean a portion or share itself, similar to qism. The term implies something that has been divided or distributed.

name: هزأ
meaning: The root هزأ (hzA) primarily means to mock, scoff, or ridicule someone. It can also mean to break something, or, less commonly, to kill camels with cold or to excite a riding beast.

name: عصي
meaning: The root ع-ص-ي primarily means to disobey, rebel, or act contrary to a command or will. It implies resistance and opposition, often against authority or divine instruction.

name: كفي
meaning: The root كفي (kfy) primarily means to suffice or be sufficient. It conveys the idea of something being enough or adequate for a purpose, fulfilling a need, or being sufficient for someone.

name: جنح
meaning: The root جنح primarily means to incline or lean towards something, physically or metaphorically. It describes a bird contracting its wings to descend, the sun setting, or night approaching.

name: مرر
meaning: The root مَرَر primarily signifies passing by, going through, or continuing. It can also denote bitterness or something that is firm and strong, like a twisted rope.

name: شمس
meaning: The root شمس primarily describes a day being sunny or the sun being intense. It also refers to a horse refusing to be ridden, becoming rebellious, or a woman abstaining from men.

name: عرش
meaning: The root 'عرش' (Ersh) primarily refers to a shaded structure, like a booth or shed, often made of natural materials.

name: برك
meaning: The root برك (brk) primarily refers to a camel's breast, the part it rests on when kneeling. It also describes a herd of camels lying down.

name: حين
meaning: The root "Hyn" refers to a period of time, often vague, but can be specific like 40 years or a glance. It can mean an appointed term, a year, or the time of an event.

name: فتح
meaning: The root فَتَحَ (fataḥa) primarily means "to open" something, like a door or a conduit. It extends to "conquer" a land, "explain" a problem, or "reveal" a secret.

name: بطل
meaning: The root بطل (bTl) primarily means something becoming false, vain, useless, or null, like a deed or an assertion. It also describes a person becoming idle or unoccupied.

name: شرب
meaning: The root شرب (shrb) primarily means "to drink" any liquid, often by gulping. It can also mean to be satisfied with drinking or, paradoxically, to be thirsty.

name: ورث
meaning: The root "wrth" primarily means "to inherit." It refers to receiving property, status, or even spiritual legacy from an ancestor or predecessor.

name: قرن
meaning: The root Q-R-N relates to things that are paired or connected. It can refer to a generation of people, an equal in age, or the side of the head where horns might grow.

name: ريب
meaning: The root ريب (ryb) primarily signifies causing disquiet or agitation of mind, leading to doubt or suspicion.

name: بلو
meaning: The root بلو (blw) primarily means to test or try, often with the intent of revealing something.

name: غفل
meaning: The root غفل (gfl) signifies being unaware, heedless, or negligent.

name: طوف
meaning: The root طوف (Twf) primarily means to go or walk around something, like circumambulating the Kaaba during Hajj.

name: سبق
meaning: The root "sbq" primarily means to precede, outstrip, or be before someone or something, in various contexts like racing, speech, or actions.

name: سخر
meaning: The root سخر (s-kh-r) primarily means to compel or constrain someone or something to work without compensation.

name: كره
meaning: The root كره (krh) signifies dislike, displeasure, or disapproval. It is the opposite of desire, contentment, or love.

name: عهد
meaning: The root عهد (Ehd) signifies a command, compact, or promise, often implying an obligation or agreement. It also refers to protection or safeguard, like a covenant of security.

name: قطع
meaning: The root QTE generally means to cut or sever. It can describe being cut off from a journey due to inability or misfortune, or speech being cut short.

name: حدث
meaning: The root Hdth primarily signifies novelty or innovation, especially in religious matters, often implying disapproval. It also refers to accidents, misfortunes, or calamities.

name: لعن
meaning: The root لعن (lEn) can refer to the base or lower part of a palm tree's raceme. This is a less common meaning, found in specialized lexicon entries.

name: ذرر
meaning: The root ذرر primarily refers to scattering or dispersing small particles, like dust or grain.

name: جهد
meaning: The root "jhd" primarily means to strive, labor, or exert oneself to the utmost. It also conveys the sense of being severely affected, distressed, or fatigued by something.

name: أوي
meaning: The root أوي primarily means to betake oneself to a place for lodging, shelter, or refuge. It implies seeking protection or a dwelling, often with the sense of returning or resorting to a safe haven.

name: ترك
meaning: The root "ترك" (taraka) primarily means to leave, abandon, or forsake something or someone, either intentionally or by necessity. It can also mean to neglect or omit.

name: قرر
meaning: The root ق ر ر primarily signifies settling, becoming firm, or remaining in a place.

name: نسي
meaning: The root نسي primarily means to forget, to omit, or to neglect something, often unintentionally. It can also imply leaving something behind or abandoning it.

name: هوي
meaning: The root هوي (hwy) primarily signifies falling or descending, often implying a downward motion or a plunge.

name: مرأ
meaning: The root مرأ (mrA) is primarily linked to the word مَرْءٌ (marʾun), which means 'a man' or 'a human being.

name: ألو
meaning: The particle "إلى" (ila) primarily signifies "to" or "until," marking the end of an extent in space or time. It can also mean "with" or "in addition to," indicating accompaniment.

name: ذنب
meaning: The root "dhnb" primarily refers to a tail, like an animal's. It can also metaphorically describe the end or last part of something, such as the "tail of a story" or the "end of a valley.

name: نكر
meaning: The root نكر (nkr) primarily means to ignore, not know, or be unacquainted with something or someone. It can also mean to deny or disacknowledge, especially a right.

name: طغي
meaning: The root طغي primarily signifies exceeding bounds, whether in water overflowing or a person transgressing limits.

name: قوي
meaning: The root Q-W-Y primarily signifies strength, power, and ability. It describes something or someone becoming strong, firm, or mighty.

name: جبل
meaning: The root جبل (jbl) primarily refers to a large company or multitude of people. It can also denote a nation or people.

name: سلط
meaning: The root سُلْطَانٌ (sultan) primarily denotes strength, power, and authority, often referring to dominion or sovereign rule. It can also signify a clear proof, evidence, or argument.

name: فلح
meaning: The root فلح (f-l-ḥ) primarily means to cleave or split, like iron or a head. It also refers to tilling land.

name: شعر
meaning: A "shi'ar" is a distinguishing mark or sign, like a battle cry or banner, used to identify groups.

name: بحر
meaning: The root "bHr" primarily refers to a large body of water, whether a sea or a great river.

name: بدل
meaning: The root بَدَلَ (badala) means to change or alter. It can refer to changing a thing's form or quality while its substance remains the same, or changing it by substituting one thing for another.

name: خشي
meaning: The root خشي primarily signifies fear, particularly a reverential fear or awe of God. It implies being humbled and submissive due to this fear, often leading to avoidance of what is feared.

name: يسر
meaning: The root يسر (ysr) primarily conveys the meaning of ease, facility, and convenience, standing in direct contrast to difficulty (عُسْرٌ).

name: ملأ
meaning: The root م ل أ primarily means "to fill." It can describe filling a vessel, satisfying the eye with beauty, or being filled with emotions like fright.

name: فوق
meaning: The root "fwq" primarily means to be above, excel, or surpass. It also describes the rising of wind from the chest (hiccuping) or a gasping at death.

name: طعم
meaning: The root طعم (TEm) primarily means "to eat" or "to taste." It can refer to consuming food or drink, or simply experiencing its flavor. The word طعام (ta'ām) means "food.

name: جوب
meaning: The root جوب (jwb) primarily means to make a hole, cut, or hollow out something, like cutting a rock or a garment. It also extends to traversing or cutting through a space, such as a land or darkness.

name: صدد
meaning: The root S-D-D primarily means to turn away, hinder, or obstruct. It implies blocking a path or preventing access, often with the sense of averting someone from a goal or purpose.

name: حزن
meaning: The root حزن (Hzn) primarily means to be or become sorrowful, sad, or grieved. It can also mean to cause someone to grieve.

name: أثم
meaning: The root أثم (Athm) primarily means to sin or commit a crime, doing what is unlawful. It also describes God reckoning something as a sin or punishing someone for it.

name: فصل
meaning: The root ف-ص-ل (fa-ṣa-la) means to separate, divide, or distinguish. It can refer to weaning a child, making a clear decision in a dispute, or a boundary separating lands.

name: حفظ
meaning: The root حفظ (Hfzh) means to keep, preserve, guard, or take care of something. It also refers to memorizing, especially the Quran, and being mindful of God's ordinances.

name: سرر
meaning: The root S-R-R primarily signifies concealment and secrecy, like hiding something within oneself or in a designated place.

name: حلل
meaning: The root H-L-L primarily signifies untying, loosening, or solving something. It also conveys the idea of descending, alighting, or becoming permissible.

name: حشر
meaning: The root حشر (Hshr) primarily means to gather or collect people, often implying driving them together or compelling them to emigrate.

name: زين
meaning: The root "zyn" means to adorn, beautify, or grace something or someone. It can refer to physical ornamentation or the embellishment of actions or speech.

name: وذر
meaning: The root وَذَرَ (wadara) means "to leave, let alone, or cease from.

name: صدر
meaning: The root S-D-R primarily refers to the front part of something, especially the chest or breast, which can metaphorically represent the mind or inner self.

name: صبح
meaning: The root صبح (SbH) primarily refers to the morning, specifically from sunrise to noon, or even from midnight to noon. It is the opposite of evening (مساء).

name: عجل
meaning: The root عجل (Ejl) primarily means to incite or cause someone to hasten or be quick. It can also mean to precede someone or something, or to do something hastily before its proper time.

name: دبر
meaning: The root دبر (dbr) primarily means to turn one's back, retreat, or go backward. It can describe physical movement, the decline of affairs, or the ending of a period like day or prayer.

name: سوع
meaning: The root سوع (swE) primarily refers to a "sāʿah," meaning an hour or an indefinite short period of time. In the Quran, it often denotes the Day of Resurrection, emphasizing its suddenness.

name: صرط
meaning: The root SrT refers to a road or way. It is also the name of a bridge over Hell, sharper than a sword and thinner than a hair, which people must cross to reach Paradise.

name: ضعف
meaning: The root DEf primarily means "the like of a thing." Over time, it evolved to mean "the like and more," implying an unlimited addition or multiplication.

name: طيب
meaning: The root "Tyb" (طيب) primarily means something is good, pleasant, pure, or clean. It can describe delightful tastes or smells, a cheerful or willing state of mind, or something becoming lawful.

name: كرم
meaning: The root كرم (krm) signifies generosity, nobility, and high esteem. It describes something as honorable, precious, or excellent, whether referring to people, animals, land, or possessions.

name: فسد
meaning: The root FSD means to make something bad, corrupt, or unsound.

name: قدم
meaning: The root QDM relates to putting something forward, advancing it, or causing it to precede. It can mean to offer, present, or prepare something beforehand.

name: أجل
meaning: The root "Ajl" primarily signifies an appointed term or period, like a deadline for a debt or the waiting period for divorced women.

name: عقل
meaning: The root عقل (Eql) primarily means to withhold or restrain. It describes binding a camel's leg, paying blood money (as camels were bound for payment), or a medicine binding the bowels.

name: نفع
meaning: The root نفع (nfE) means to profit, benefit, or be useful to someone or something. It can also describe a remedy being good or beneficial for a disease.

name: عدد
meaning: The root ع د د primarily means to count, number, or reckon. It also conveys the idea of preparing something for a specific purpose or time, and can refer to a number or quantity itself.

name: تحت
meaning: The root ت-ح-ت primarily denotes "beneath," "below," or "under," contrasting with "above." It can be an adverb or a noun, often indeclinable when its associated noun is suppressed.

name: ندي
meaning: The root ندي primarily signifies moisture, dew, or dampness.

name: حمل
meaning: The root "Hml" primarily means to bear, carry, or convey. It extends to a woman becoming pregnant, a tree bearing fruit, or taking on responsibility for a debt.

name: روح
meaning: The root "rwH" primarily describes wind-related phenomena: a day being windy, or a place becoming cool and airy.

name: خبر
meaning: The root خَبَرَ (khabra) primarily means to know something deeply, especially its inner state. It also signifies trying or testing something to gain knowledge or experience.

name: نسو
meaning: The root نسو (nsw) refers to a vein or nerve, often associated with the thigh and leg. It's known as the sciatic vein/nerve, and the term can also denote sciatica or hip-gout.

name: دور
meaning: The root دَارٌ (dār) primarily denotes a house, mansion, or any place of abode, often including a courtyard.

name: ضرب
meaning: The root "Drb" primarily means to strike or beat. It can also mean to set up (a tent), coin (money), seal (a writing), or impose (a tax).

name: فسق
meaning: The root ف س ق (fsq) primarily means to go forth from something in a bad or corrupt manner, like a date coming out of its skin.

name: قري
meaning: The primary meaning of QRY relates to collecting or gathering water, often in a basin or reservoir.

name: بيت
meaning: A "bayt" is primarily a tent, often made of hair or wool, varying in size and number of poles. It also refers to any house, dwelling, or chamber, including structures of clay or stone.

name: نهي
meaning: The root ن-ه-ي primarily signifies prohibition, forbidding, or restraining someone from doing something. It also conveys the idea of reaching an end or limit, as in the "end" of a matter.

name: فري
meaning: The root فري (fry) describes a lamb or its skin, or a kid's skin, being roasted until its upper parts become dried up. This term is found in Q. 3.

name: ذهب
meaning: The root ذَهَبَ primarily means "to go" or "to depart." It can also mean to go away with something, to remove it, or to cause it to disappear.

name: صوب
meaning: The root Swb primarily means to descend or go down. It also signifies hitting a target, like an arrow, or an event befalling someone.

name: زكو
meaning: The root زكو (zkw) primarily means increase, augmentation, and purity, often with divine blessing. It also signifies purification, good conduct, and religious service.

name: ظنن
meaning: The root ظنن primarily means to think, imagine, or suppose something to be true, often with an element of doubt or uncertainty.

name: مسس
meaning: The root مَسَسَ primarily means to touch, feel, or handle something with the hand. It can also imply affecting or reaching something, like an affliction or a blessing.

name: ظهر
meaning: The root "zhhr" primarily refers to the back, contrasting with the belly. It can metaphorically describe having a large or small household, or being forgotten/neglected.

name: ردد
meaning: The root ردد primarily means to return, repel, or send back. It encompasses the idea of turning something back, whether physically or metaphorically, and can also imply repetition or reconsideration.

name: فتن
meaning: The root فتن (ftn) primarily means to burn or melt something, like gold, to purify or test it.

name: عود
meaning: The root عود (Ewd) primarily means "to return" or "to revert to a state." It can also signify "to become" or "to begin.

name: غيب
meaning: The root غيب (ghyb) primarily means to be absent, hidden, or concealed. It describes something out of sight or beyond perception, like the setting sun or a person who has journeyed away.

name: زيد
meaning: Ziyādah (زيادة) means an increase, addition, or augmentation. It refers to something extra or superfluous. For example, "letters of augmentation" are letters added to a root word.

name: سحر
meaning: The root سحر (sahar) primarily refers to the time just before daybreak, the last part of the night.

name: قضي
meaning: The root قضي primarily means to decide, judge, or decree something. It also conveys the sense of completing, finishing, or fulfilling a matter, often with an element of finality or inevitability.

name: موه
meaning: The root موه (mwh) primarily means to coat something, like silvering or gilding.

name: خسر
meaning: The root خسر (khsr) primarily means to lose, suffer loss, or be deceived, often in trade or personal acquisitions like health or intellect. It can also mean to err, go astray, or perish.

name: كسب
meaning: The root كسب (kasaba) primarily means to collect or gain, acquire, or earn something, like wealth.

name: تلو
meaning: The root "tlw" primarily means to follow, whether physically or figuratively, like the moon following the sun.

name: ذوق
meaning: The root ذوق (dhwq) primarily means "to taste" food or drink. By extension, it signifies perceiving or experiencing something, not limited to the tongue.

name: وكل
meaning: The root وَكَلَ (wakala) primarily means to rely upon someone, confide in them, or submit oneself to them.

name: عين
meaning: The root "عَيْنٌ" (Eyn) is a homonym with over a hundred meanings, seventeen of which appear in the Quran. Its primary meaning is "the eye," the organ of sight.

name: عرف
meaning: The root عرف (Erf) primarily means 'acknowledgment' or 'confession.' It also signifies 'goodness,' 'beneficence,' or 'what is deemed good/approved.

name: هلك
meaning: The root هلك (hlk) primarily means to perish or be destroyed. It can also describe a gradual perishing due to grief.

name: يمن
meaning: The root يمن (ymn) primarily refers to the right side or the south. It also signifies a covenant or oath, often made by God.

name: جري
meaning: The root جرى primarily signifies flowing, running, or moving swiftly, like water or a ship. It also denotes the passage of time or events, and the act of happening or occurring.

name: وفي
meaning: The root WFY primarily means to fulfill, complete, or perform something fully and perfectly.

name: بعث
meaning: The root بعث (b-ʿ-th) primarily means to remove restraint, enabling free action. It signifies sending someone or something, rousing or exciting, and awakening from sleep.

name: رضو
meaning: The root رضو (rDw) primarily means to be pleased, content, or satisfied with someone or something. It can also mean to approve, like, or choose.

name: متع
meaning: The root مَتَاعٌ (matāʿ) refers to anything useful or advantageous, like goods, household items, or provisions. It can also denote enjoyment or the necessities of life.

name: جرم
meaning: The root جَرَمَ (jrm) primarily means "to cut" or "to cut off," especially in the context of harvesting fruits like dates or shearing wool. It also signifies "to gain" or "to earn.

name: حمد
meaning: The root حمد (Hmd) means to praise, commend, or speak well of someone, often for their volitional actions or qualities. It implies admiration, honor, and sometimes humility.

name: سكن
meaning: The root "skn" primarily means to be still, quiet, or at rest after motion. It also conveys the idea of trusting or relying on something for peace of mind, and dwelling or inhabiting a place.

name: فرق
meaning: The root فَرَقَ (faraqa) means to separate, distinguish, or divide.

name: رجل
meaning: The root "رجل" (rijl) primarily refers to the leg or foot of humans and animals. It can also denote a part or portion of something, like half of a roasted sheep or a pair of saddlebags.

name: وحد
meaning: The root وَحْد (wHd) primarily means "one" or "single," signifying something without subdivision. It's often interchangeable with أَحَدٌ, especially for God, but with distinct usage patterns.

name: علو
meaning: The root عَلَى (ʿalā) primarily denotes superiority, both physical (e.g., "upon the roof") and abstract (e.g., "a debt is upon him").

name: توب
meaning: The root توب (twb) primarily means to return. When a person "returns" (تَابَ), it signifies repenting from sin, expressing regret, and turning back to God.

name: شكر
meaning: The root شكر (shkr) primarily means to thank, be grateful, or acknowledge beneficence.

name: بأس
meaning: The root بأس (bAs) primarily conveys the idea of strength, courage, and severity, often in the context of war or adversity. It can also denote distress, misfortune, or a state of being in need.

name: ضرر
meaning: The root ضَرَر primarily signifies harm, injury, or damage, whether physical or moral. It encompasses causing detriment to someone or something, and experiencing such adversity.

name: وحي
meaning: The root W-H-Y primarily signifies a swift, secret, and subtle communication or indication.

name: كلم
meaning: The root كَلَمَ (kalam) refers to speech, words, or discourse, whether little or much, singular or plural. It encompasses spoken language, sayings, and even a grammatical sentence.

name: حرم
meaning: The root حُرْمَةٌ (ḥurmah) signifies something forbidden, sacred, or inviolable. It also denotes reverence, respect, and honor due to a person or thing.

name: وجه
meaning: The root وَجْه (wjh) primarily refers to the 'face' or 'countenance,' often symbolizing the whole self, as in 'resigning oneself to God.

name: زوج
meaning: The root زَوْج (zawj) primarily means one of a pair or a couple, or a sort/kind of thing. It can refer to a husband or wife, a consociate, or a like/fellow. In arithmetic, it signifies an even number.

name: عرض
meaning: The root عَرَضَ (ErD) primarily means "to be or become broad/wide." It also signifies something appearing or becoming apparent, especially from afar, or an event befalling someone.

name: غني
meaning: The root غني primarily signifies self-sufficiency and independence from others. It conveys being rich, content, and having no need. It also relates to dwelling or residing in a place.

name: ألم
meaning: The root ألم (Alm) signifies experiencing pain or aching. It describes a state of suffering, whether of a person or a body part like the belly or head.

name: بلغ
meaning: The root "بلغ" (balagha) primarily means to reach, attain, or arrive at a destination, time, or state. It encompasses the idea of reaching the utmost point or full maturity, whether physically (e.g.

name: قلل
meaning: The root Q-L-L primarily means to be or become little, small, or few. It can also refer to making something little, or to a small quantity or number.

name: عقب
meaning: The root 'Eqb' primarily refers to the heel of a human foot. It extends to mean the end or last part of something, like the end of a month or the consequence of an action.

name: نجو
meaning: The root نجو (njw) primarily means to escape or be safe. It can also describe swiftness, outstripping others, or, in a more crude sense, voiding excrement or breaking wind.

name: شطن
meaning: The root شطن (shTn) refers to anything excessively proud, rebellious, or distant from truth. It can describe humans, jinn, or even certain serpents.

name: قرأ
meaning: A *muqriʾ* can refer to a menstruating woman, or one who has become pure after menstruation. It also denotes someone who teaches others to read or recite, especially the Qur'an.

name: سوي
meaning: The root سوي (swy) primarily means to make even, level, or straight. It also conveys the idea of completing, perfecting, or fashioning something in due proportion.

name: ولد
meaning: The root "wld" primarily refers to birth and offspring. A "walīd" is a newborn, infant, or young child. It can also denote a boy, youth, or servant, especially one born into servitude.

name: مول
meaning: The root MWL primarily refers to "wealth" or "property." It encompasses anything one owns, from currency to animals like camels, which were a significant part of Arab wealth.

name: أخو
meaning: The root أخو (akhw) primarily means "brother," referring to a male sibling by birth or fosterage. It can also metaphorically mean a friend, companion, or associate.

name: سجد
meaning: Sujud primarily means to be humble/submissive, often involving bending down. In prayer, it's prostrating with the forehead on the ground.

name: ليل
meaning: The root ليل (layl) primarily refers to night. Related phrases include "daughters of the night" for wishes and anxieties, and "son of the night" for a thief or traveler.

name: أحد
meaning: The root AHd primarily means "one" or "first." It's often interchangeable with "wahid" but has specific uses. For God, "Ahad" means "The One, the Sole," emphasizing indivisibility.

name: كيف
meaning: The root كَيَّفَ (kayyafa) and its derivatives, used by Muslim theologians, mean to specify something by its quality. These terms were formed by analogy, not directly heard from ancient Arabs.

name: ليس
meaning: The root "laysa" (ليس) is a verb of negation, meaning "is not." It functions like "kana" (كان), making the subject nominative and the predicate accusative.

name: حبب
meaning: The root HBB primarily conveys the meaning of love, affection, and being beloved. It also encompasses the idea of producing seeds or grains, and by extension, becoming pregnant or having a full udder.

name: نفق
meaning: The root نفق (nfq) primarily relates to something being in demand, selling well, or becoming brisk, like a market.

name: خلد
meaning: The root خلد (khld) signifies permanence or eternity, as in "Paradise" (دار الخلد). It also refers to a type of bracelet or earring.

name: دين
meaning: The root "dyn" primarily means to be obedient, abased, or submissive. It also signifies following a religion, especially Islam. Interestingly, it can also mean to be disobedient or mighty.

name: بعض
meaning: The root بَعْض (bED) primarily means "some" or "a part/portion" of something, whether small or large. It can refer to people or things.

name: سبح
meaning: The root "sbH" relates to declaring God's absolute perfection and freedom from any imperfection. "SubhanAllah" means "Glory be to God," expressing wonder and praise, acknowledging His transcendence.

name: صحب
meaning: A "ṣāḥib" is a companion, associate, or friend. It also means a lord, master, or owner of something. In the Quran, it refers to companions in Paradise or Hell, and recording angels.

name: صلو
meaning: The root صلو (Slw) primarily means to pray, supplicate, or perform the ritual prayer (ṣalāh). When God or angels "pray" for someone, it means to bless, have mercy, or magnify them.

name: أذن
meaning: The root أذن (Adhn) primarily refers to the ear, an organ of sense. It can also metaphorically describe a person who is a good listener or who readily believes what is said to them.

name: بغي
meaning: The root ب-غ-ي primarily means to seek or desire something, often with an implication of exceeding bounds or acting unjustly. It can denote transgression, rebellion, or seeking something wrongfully.

name: قرب
meaning: The root "qarab" primarily means to be or become near. It can refer to physical proximity, closeness in rank, or family relationship.

name: فضل
meaning: The root فضل (fDl) primarily signifies excess, superabundance, or superiority, often implying a positive quality like grace or bounty, especially from God.

name: صبر
meaning: The root "Sbr" primarily means to confine or restrain. This can apply to oneself, leading to the meaning of patience or endurance, especially in the face of adversity.

name: أجر
meaning: The root أجر (Ajr) primarily means recompensing or rewarding someone for their actions. It also refers to hiring someone or letting something for hire, like a house.

name: شدد
meaning: The root شَدَدَ primarily conveys the idea of strengthening, making firm, or binding tightly. It implies intensity, severity, or becoming hard and robust.

name: وجد
meaning: The root "wjd" primarily means "to find" or "to discover," encompassing physical, intellectual, and sensory perception. It can also mean "to become rich" or "to be angry with someone.

name: أكل
meaning: The root أكل primarily means "to eat" or "to consume." It extends to various metaphorical uses, such as consuming wealth, time, or even defaming someone.

name: فعل
meaning: The root فِعْل (fiʿl) refers to a deed, action, or motion, encompassing any origination or effect produced by an agent. It's a broad term for what has become apparent due to a motive.

name: نهر
meaning: The root نَهَرَ (nahara) primarily describes water flowing strongly, like a river, or blood gushing. It also means to dig a river channel.

name: حسب
meaning: The root HSB primarily means to number, count, or calculate. It also conveys the sense of thinking or supposing something to be true.

name: رجع
meaning: The root رَجَعَ primarily means "to return" or "go back" to a place, state, or action. It can also signify repentance, retracting a statement, or taking back a gift.

name: أبو
meaning: The root "أبو" (abw) primarily means "father" or "ancestor." It has various declensions and plural forms, including "آباء" (ābāʾ).

name: أمم
meaning: The root أمم primarily signifies heading towards a place or person with an intention. It also relates to leadership, being in front, or being a model to be followed.

name: رزق
meaning: Rizq (رزق) refers to provisions or sustenance, especially from God. It encompasses both material things like food and immaterial blessings like knowledge.

name: جزي
meaning: The root جزي primarily means to recompense or reward, often for good or evil deeds. It implies a return or requital for actions, whether positive or negative.

name: يدي
meaning: The root يدي (ydy) primarily refers to the hand, signifying power, possession, or a gift. It can also denote a side, direction, or a means by which something is accomplished.

name: دخل
meaning: The root دَخَلَ primarily means "to enter" or "go in," the opposite of "to exit." It can refer to entering a physical space, joining a group, or starting an action.

name: خوف
meaning: The root خ و ف primarily means "to fear" or "be afraid." It can also mean "to know" or "to think," as seen in Quranic verses like "if a woman fears ill-treatment from her husband.

name: نذر
meaning: The root نذر (ndhr) primarily means to make a vow or impose something as obligatory upon oneself, often to God. It can also mean to dedicate a child to religious service.

name: نظر
meaning: The root "nzhr" primarily means to look at or towards something with one's eye. It can also mean to consider or investigate something intellectually, or to face something.

name: خلف
meaning: The root خَلَف (khlf) primarily means to come after, follow, or succeed someone or something. It also conveys the sense of being a substitute or replacing what was lost.

name: سأل
meaning: The root سأل primarily means to ask, inquire, or question. It also encompasses the idea of begging or requesting something, often with a sense of seeking information or a favor.

name: طوع
meaning: The root طوع (TwE) signifies submission, obedience, and willingness. It describes someone who acts voluntarily, not under compulsion, and is compliant or tractable.

name: بشر
meaning: The root "b-sh-r" primarily means to strip or pare something, revealing its underlying surface. This can refer to skin, the ground, or even a mustache.

name: أول
meaning: The root "Awl" primarily means to return or revert to an original state, source, or result. It can describe things decreasing, thickening (like milk or oil), or an affair reaching a certain outcome.

name: رود
meaning: The root ر و د (rwd) conveys gentleness, slowness, or a leisurely manner. It can describe walking softly or acting patiently.

name: أهل
meaning: Ahl refers to the people of a house, town, or country, and a man's family or cohabitants. It can also mean one's kinsfolk, followers, or those who share a religion or profession.

name: قدر
meaning: The root Q-D-R relates to measuring, proportioning, and determining. It can mean to compute the quantity of something, to make a garment to measure, or for God to decree or ordain.

name: قتل
meaning: The root Q-T-L, beyond its common meaning of "to kill," can also mean "to know something thoroughly." It implies a deep, complete understanding, as if one has mastered the knowledge of that thing.

name: شهد
meaning: The root شهد (shhd) refers to witnessing, observing, or being present. A "shahid" is a witness, one who testifies to what they've seen or know, or someone who is present.

name: جمع
meaning: The root جَمْع (jmE) signifies collecting or gathering. It refers to a collection, assembly, or multitude of people or things.

name: عظم
meaning: The root عظم (Ezhm) primarily means "to be great in bone," then metaphorically "to be great" in general, whether physically or intellectually.

name: سلم
meaning: The root "slm" primarily conveys safety, security, and freedom from imperfections or evils.

name: نعم
meaning: The root نعم (nEm) primarily signifies a benefit, favor, or blessing from God, encompassing boons, bounties, and grace. It also denotes ease, plenty, and welfare, often contrasted with hardship.

name: دنو
meaning: The root دَنَا (danā) means to be or become near, to approach. It can refer to physical proximity, time, or even kinship.

name: وعد
meaning: The root وَعَدَ (waʿada) means "to promise," encompassing both good and evil. When used for good, it's a promise; for evil, it's a threat.

name: لقي
meaning: The root L-Q-Y primarily signifies meeting or encountering, whether intentionally or by chance.

name: نصر
meaning: The root نصر (nSr) primarily means to aid, assist, or strengthen someone, especially against an enemy or injustice. It also conveys the sense of making someone victorious or avenging them.

name: بصر
meaning: The root بصر (bSr) relates to mental perception, understanding, and insight, often contrasted with physical sight. It also signifies knowledge, firm belief, and constancy in religion.

name: دون
meaning: The root "dwn" primarily describes something low, inferior, or beneath, whether in status, quality, or physical position. It can also mean "nearer than" or "before" in terms of proximity or sequence.

name: موت
meaning: The root "mwt" primarily means "to die," the opposite of living.

name: شرك
meaning: Shirk (شِرْكٌ) means sharing or partnership, like in land or marriage. It also refers to a share or portion.

name: صدق
meaning: The root S-D-Q primarily means to speak the truth, to be veracious, or to prove true. It encompasses sincerity in words, actions, and intentions, contrasting with falsehood.

name: غير
meaning: The root غَيْر (ghayr) means "other than" or "not." It is used to qualify nouns, often governing the following noun in the genitive case.

name: مثل
meaning: The root مَثَلٌ (mathal) signifies a description through comparison, often used for important or remarkable states.

name: بني
meaning: The root B-N-Y primarily signifies building, constructing, or erecting something, often with the sense of raising it up.

name: نبأ
meaning: The root نَبَأَ (nbA) primarily means to utter a low sound or bark. It also conveys meanings of being exalted, assaulting, or going forth from a land.

name: سوأ
meaning: The root سوأ (swA) primarily means to be or become evil, bad, or unseemly. It can also mean to displease, grieve, or vex someone.

name: كبر
meaning: The root كبر (kbr) signifies greatness, age, or importance. أَكْبَرُ means greater/greatest in body, rank, or age. It can also mean older/oldest.

name: قلب
meaning: The root "قلب" (qlb) primarily means to alter, change, or invert something, turning it upside down or inside out. It can refer to physical actions like turning over bread or earth, or examining goods.

name: تبع
meaning: The root "tbE" primarily means to follow, go after, or pursue. It can imply literally following someone's footsteps, or figuratively following their actions, guidance, or even pursuing a debt.

name: خرج
meaning: The root خَرَجَ (khrj) primarily means to go or come out, to issue forth. The form أَخْرَجَ (akhraja) means to make something go out, to expel, extract, or produce it.

name: كثر
meaning: The root كثر (kthr) signifies abundance, whether in quantity, number, or frequency. It describes something as much, many, numerous, or copious.

name: سمع
meaning: The root "smE" primarily relates to hearing. It can mean the act of hearing, the faculty of hearing (the sense of the ear), or the ear itself.

name: سبل
meaning: The root سبل (sbl) primarily means "to let loose or hang down," often referring to garments dragging on the ground.

name: حيي
meaning: The root حيي primarily signifies life, living, and being alive.

name: ضلل
meaning: The root ضلل primarily means to go astray, deviate from the right path, or err, whether intentionally or unintentionally. It can also imply being lost or perishing.

name: صلح
meaning: The root صلح (SlH) means to make something good, right, or sound. It encompasses rectifying, correcting, improving, or restoring something to a proper state.

name: نور
meaning: The root "nwr" primarily refers to "fire" (نَارٌ), its flame, and heat. It can metaphorically signify war, evil, or even counsel. It also denotes a brand mark on camels, used for identification.

name: حسن
meaning: The root HSN generally means "good," "beautiful," or "pleasing." It describes something intrinsically good, like faith, or extrinsically good, like fighting for a cause.

name: خير
meaning: The root خَيْر (khyr) signifies "good" in various forms: moral, physical, or beneficial. It can refer to wealth, horses, or general well-being.

name: دعو
meaning: The root دعو (daʿaw) primarily means to call, invite, or summon. It also encompasses praying or supplicating to God, whether for good or against someone.

name: حكم
meaning: The root H-K-M primarily means to prevent or restrain someone from evil or undesired actions. It also refers to curbing a horse or controlling events.

name: عند
meaning: The root `عند` (ʿinda) primarily functions as an adverb of place and time, meaning "at," "near," "with," or "in the presence of.

name: ملك
meaning: The root ملك (mlk) primarily means to possess or own something, often with exclusive rights and the ability to exercise authority over it.

name: جنن
meaning: The root جَنّ (jann) primarily means to cover, conceal, or hide. It refers to something that is veiled or becomes dark, like the night.

name: غفر
meaning: The root غفر primarily means to cover or conceal. From this, it extends to God covering sins, meaning to forgive or pardon them.

name: ولي
meaning: The root W-L-Y primarily signifies being near, close, or following. It extends to concepts of guardianship, protection, friendship, and authority, often implying a close relationship or succession.

name: خلق
meaning: The root "khlq" primarily means to measure or proportion something, like a hide for sewing.

name: بعد
meaning: The root بَعُدَ primarily means to be or become distant, remote, or far off. It can also signify perishing or dying. In some contexts, it expresses improbability or reaching an extreme degree.

name: أمر
meaning: The root أَمَرَ (amara) primarily means "to command" or "order." It can also signify "to have authority" or "to govern.

name: وقي
meaning: The root وقي (wqy) primarily means to protect, guard, or preserve something from harm or danger. It encompasses the idea of shielding oneself or others, and being cautious or wary.

name: أخر
meaning: The root أخر (Akhr) primarily means "last," "aftermost," or "end," contrasting with "first." It refers to the final part of something, like the end of a day or life.

name: أخذ
meaning: The root "Akhdh" primarily means "to take" or "to seize." It can also mean "to receive," "to accept," "to take possession of," or "to take away.

name: عبد
meaning: The root عبد (Ebd) primarily refers to a male slave or servant. It also denotes a worshipper of God or other deities, and can simply mean a human being.

name: نزل
meaning: The root نزل primarily signifies descent, like milk into an udder. It also means to lodge or host someone, providing refuge.

name: كذب
meaning: The root كذب primarily means "to lie" or "utter a falsehood," whether intentionally or not.

name: جيأ
meaning: The root ج-ي-أ primarily means "to come" or "to be present." It can refer to physical arrival, the occurrence of events, or even bringing something forth.

name: حقق
meaning: The root HQQ primarily signifies truth, reality, and certainty. It denotes that which is established as true, right, or due.

name: ذكر
meaning: The root ذَكَرَ (dhakara) primarily means to remember or recall something, especially after forgetting. It also encompasses the act of mentioning, telling, or relating something verbally.

name: هدي
meaning: The root هدي (hdy) primarily means to guide or lead someone on the right path, often with the implication of showing the way or directing.

name: نفس
meaning: Nafs (نفس) refers to the soul, spirit, or vital principle, often contrasted with rūḥ (روح). It can also mean a thing's self or essence.

name: كتب
meaning: The root كَتَبَ primarily means "to write." It also signifies "to prescribe, appoint, or ordain," especially by God, making something obligatory.

name: قبل
meaning: The root قبل (qbl) primarily means to face or come forward.

name: ظلم
meaning: The root "ظلم" (ẓalam) primarily means to put something in a wrong place or to transgress proper limits. It encompasses doing wrong, acting unjustly, or causing loss.

name: رأي
meaning: The root رَأَى primarily means to see with the eye or perceive with the mind.

name: جعل
meaning: The root "جعل" (jaʿala) is a versatile verb meaning to make, create, or appoint.

name: عمل
meaning: The root عَمَل (Eml) refers to an intentional action or deed, often implying effort or difficulty. It's more specific than a general "act" (فِعْل) and is typically attributed to animate beings.

name: أنس
meaning: The root أنس (Ans) primarily refers to mankind, often contrasted with jinn. It also signifies a close, chosen friend or companion.

name: عذب
meaning: The root عذب (Edhb) primarily refers to motes or particles floating on water, or the green substance (طُحْلُب) found in water.

name: سمو
meaning: The root "smw" relates to "ism" (name). An ism is a sign or word that conveys knowledge of a thing, distinguishing it. It's derived from "samawtu" or "sumuww," meaning to elevate or make known.

name: كلل
meaning: The root K-L-L primarily signifies weariness, dullness, or becoming blunt, like a sword losing its sharpness.

name: رسل
meaning: A *rasūl* is primarily a messenger, one sent with a message, often an apostle of God. The word implies a continuous relaying of tidings, like camels following one another.

name: يوم
meaning: The root "ywm" primarily refers to a period of time, often a day from sunrise to sunset, or from dawn to sunset. It can also denote an event or accident, a day's journey, or a battle/conflict.

name: أرض
meaning: The root "أرض" (ArD) primarily refers to the earth, ground, or land. It can denote the planet, a country, or a piece of soil.

name: شيأ
meaning: The root شَيَأ primarily means a "thing" or "something." It refers to an entity, object, or matter, whether concrete or abstract, that exists or is conceived. It can also denote a state or affair.

name: بين
meaning: The root "byn" has two opposite meanings: separation and union. It can refer to the distance between things or people, or the state of affairs between them, whether good or bad.

name: كفر
meaning: The root كفر (kfr) primarily means to cover or conceal something. From this, it extends to covering up or denying a favor, leading to the meaning of ingratitude.

name: أتي
meaning: The root أتي primarily signifies coming, arriving, or bringing something. It can also denote happening, occurring, or being given.

name: أيي
meaning: The primary meaning of اِسْم (ism) is a name, or that by which a thing is known. It can also refer to a quality or characteristic that distinguishes something.

name: قوم
meaning: The root قوم (qwm) primarily means to stand, rise, or be erect. It extends to undertaking or managing affairs, maintaining someone, or even signifying the establishment of something.

name: أمن
meaning: The root أمن (Amn) primarily conveys security, safety, and freedom from fear. It also extends to concepts of trust, confidence, and being trustworthy.

name: علم
meaning: The root "Elm" (علم) primarily means "to know" or "to be acquainted with." It signifies true, certain knowledge, often implying a deeper understanding than mere acquaintance.

name: ربب
meaning: The root ر ب ب (rbb) primarily means to be master, owner, or lord. It also conveys the sense of nurturing, rearing, or bringing up something to maturity.

name: كون
meaning: The root "kwn" primarily means "he/it was." It's an "incomplete" verb, meaning it needs a predicate to form a complete thought, like "Zeyd was standing.

name: قول
meaning: The root ق و ل (qawala) primarily means "to say." It can also mean to assert belief, make a sign with a body part, or even to lie.

name: أله
meaning: Allah (اللّٰهُ) is the proper name for the one true God, encompassing all attributes of perfection.
